title: Source vetting: verify reuse terms + record provenance for budgeting sources
context: Every factual claim in the curriculum must be backed by an authoritative, openly-reusable source with verified reuse terms — public domain (e.g., U.S. federal works), CC-BY, CC-BY-SA, or equivalent — and recorded provenance including license/legal-status, retrieval date, lastVerified, and validUntil. Commercial finance content and proprietary rate/product/comparison data are categorically excluded. This vetting task covers the sources needed for the M1 Budgeting module and must be completed and expert-signed-off before content drafting begins.
objective: Identify, vet, and record provenance for all authoritative, openly-licensed sources needed for the Budgeting-basics module, producing a machine-readable source registry entry for each source with verified reuse terms and staleness fields.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 3 authoritative open sources for budgeting basics are identified (e.g., government consumer-finance agencies, nonprofit financial-counseling bodies, OER repositories) with public-domain or open-license status confirmed
Reuse terms verified and recorded per source: source name, publisher, jurisdiction, citation, URL, retrieval date, license/legal-status note, lastVerified, and validUntil
No all-rights-reserved commercial finance content; no commercial rate/product/comparison data included
CC-BY-SA sources are tagged for share-alike inheritance so they are segregated/labeled correctly in the curriculum
Source registry entries conform to the content/lesson JSON Schema (task 004) sources[] format
Expert sign-off (credentialed financial educator) recorded before sources are enabled for content use
output: A machine-readable source registry (data/sources/budgeting-sources.json) with verified provenance records for all budgeting-module sources, conforming to the sources[] schema and ready for citation in content tasks.
